---
title: "elsa"
subtitle: "An isochronal model for ice-sheet layer tracing"
toc: false
---

**elsa** — the Englacial Layer Simulation Architecture — advects a stack of
isochronal layers through an ice-sheet domain. Its vertical axis is *time*: each
layer is bounded by two isochrones and never exchanges mass with its
neighbours, so vertical numerical diffusion is eliminated by construction. elsa
is driven by a host ice-sheet model, which supplies horizontal velocity, ice
thickness, and surface and basal mass balance, and which elsa never modifies.

The method is that of [Born (2017)](https://doi.org/10.1017/jog.2016.111),
[Born and Robinson (2021)](https://doi.org/10.5194/tc-15-4539-2021) and
[Rieckh et al. (2024)](https://doi.org/10.5194/gmd-17-6987-2024). This
implementation is a reimplementation of the Bergen ELSA v2.0, not a fork; see
[Design](design.qmd) for where it departs from the published scheme, and why.

::: {.callout-note}
This is a from-scratch reimplementation under active development. The library,
its public API, NetCDF output, restart and the full benchmark suite are in
place. The Yelmox coupling is being wired separately.
:::

## The interface

A host model needs one module and three calls:

```fortran
use elsa

type(elsa_class) :: els

call elsa_init(els,"elsa.nml","elsa",time,time_end,xc,yc,zeta_aa,H_ice,"acx_acy")

call elsa_update(els,time,H_ice,ux,uy,smb,bmb)   ! every host timestep

call elsa_end(els)
```

`elsa_update` takes an absolute time, works out its own coupling interval,
decides for itself whether an update is due, and keeps its own previous-step ice
thickness — so the host calls it unconditionally and manages none of elsa's
bookkeeping. Host fields may be single or double precision; elsa converts at the
boundary. `stagger` declares where the host's velocity samples sit — `"acx_acy"`
for staggered faces (Yelmo), `"aa"` for cell-centred.

## What is new relative to v2.0

::: {.pkg-grid}

::: {.pkg-card}
### Correct upwinding
Each cell face is upwinded on its own velocity. v2.0 chose the direction for
both x-faces from the sign of one, which put a negative entry on the matrix
diagonal in convergent cells and made the solve diverge. Three stability guards
existed only to mask this; all are deleted. See [physics](benchmarks/physics.qmd).
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### No linear solver
The advection is explicit and sub-stepped, so there is no LIS and no system
solve. fesm-utils is elsa's only dependency. v2.0 required LIS, and required you
to build and locate it.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### Robust interpolation
Any host grid maps onto elsa's grid through one separable, exact stencil, built
once at init. `grid_factor` may be any real ≥ 1, not just an integer divisor.
See [interp](benchmarks/interp.qmd).
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### Restart
A run stopped and restarted is bit-identical to the run that never stopped. v2.0
could not restart at all. See [greenland](benchmarks/greenland.qmd).
:::

:::

## Benchmarks

Every claim above is checked. `make check` runs the Fortran benchmarks, which
assert structural properties and exit nonzero on failure; `make validate` then
runs the Julia analysis, which checks the physics against closed-form answers
and draws the figures.

| Benchmark | What it establishes |
|---|---|
| [physics](benchmarks/physics.qmd) | advection is stable, positive and mass-conserving at any CFL |
| [interp](benchmarks/interp.qmd) | the grid maps are exact |
| [column](benchmarks/column.qmd) | isochrones follow the Nye analytic solution |
| [greenland](benchmarks/greenland.qmd) | it runs on a real 3D ice sheet, and restarts exactly |

## Install

elsa is a [configme](https://github.com/fesmc/configme) package:

```bash
configme install elsa --only     # elsa + fesm-utils
```
